Container.Name: unifi-switchboard
Container.Overview: UniFi Switchboard is a Lightweight web UI to enable and disable UniFi zone-based firewall policies from any browser on your LAN. Targets the UCG-Max (firmware 10.x) firewall-policies API. Log in with your APP_PASSWORD and toggle your custom rules on or off instantly. Only user-created policies are shown; system defaults are hidden.
Container.Changes:
### 1.1.0 - Optional `UNIFI_API_KEY` variable — enables correct policy display order (matches UniFi UI) - Policies sorted per zone pair using the integration ordering endpoint - Generate API key in Network → Integrations → API Keys
### 1.0.0 - Initial release - Zone-based firewall policy toggling via UniFi firewall-policies API - Password-protected web UI with 8-hour sessions - Filters to user-created policies only (hides predefined system rules) - Security headers, timing-safe password comparison, input validation

Config.Name: Port
Config.Description: Port to access the UniFi Switchboard web UI

Config.Name: UNIFI_URL
Config.Description: Base URL of your UniFi Cloud Gateway — no trailing slash (e.g. https://192.168.1.1)

Config.Name: APP_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Password used to log into this web UI

Config.Name: SECRET_KEY
Config.Description: Random secret for Flask sessions. Generate one with: python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"

Config.Name: UNIFI_SITE
Config.Description: UniFi site name — leave as 'default' for single-site setups. Change if you use multiple sites or a custom site name.

Config.Name: UNIFI_API_KEY
Config.Description: Local UniFi API key — enables correct rule evaluation order display. Generate in Network > Integrations > API Keys.

Config.Name: TRUST_PROXY
Config.Description: Set to true when running behind a reverse proxy or Cloudflare Tunnel — enables ProxyFix and Secure cookie flag
